clinical_coherence.notes: 41F right-handed East Asian with MRI-negative temporal lobe epilepsy. The clinical presentation features gustatory aura (metallic taste) and ictal speech arrest localizing to dominant temporal lobe. No history of febrile seizures, making the etiology more uncertain. Comorbid migraine (2-3/month) creates additional diagnostic complexity. The neurological exam is appropriately normal. Brother with childhood absence epilepsy adds realistic genetic context.

tool_output_consistency.notes: EEG shows left temporal sharp waves. MRI is NEGATIVE (no hippocampal sclerosis or structural lesion) - this is the key complicating factor. Video-EEG captures seizures with left temporal onset. Neuropsych shows material-specific verbal memory deficit consistent with left temporal dysfunction. The MRI-negative status makes this a genuinely more complex case.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G40.109 correct. The case correctly identifies MRI-negative TLE as having lower surgical success rates. Differential includes focal cortical dysplasia (may be missed on MRI), low-grade glioma, and autoimmune encephalitis. Discussion of advanced imaging (7T MRI, MEG) for surgical candidates is appropriate.

difficulty_calibration.notes: MRI-negative TLE is genuinely more complex than MRI-positive cases. The diagnostic and management uncertainty (lower surgical success without a visible lesion) is well-calibrated for moderate difficulty.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 41F East Asian with MRI-negative TLE is plausible. The absence of febrile seizure history is consistent with non-MTS etiologies like focal cortical dysplasia.

educational_value.notes: Excellent teaching case for MRI-negative temporal lobe epilepsy, gustatory aura localization, ictal speech arrest in dominant hemisphere epilepsy, and the implications of MRI-negative status for surgical candidacy. Advanced imaging considerations (7T MRI, MEG) are clinically relevant.

strengths: MRI-negative TLE is an important and underrepresented clinical scenario
Gustatory aura and ictal speech arrest are distinct localizing features
Discussion of advanced imaging (7T MRI, MEG) for surgical planning is current and relevant
Neuropsych findings appropriately lateralize despite negative MRI
